what: Calculate Shannon's entropy, ENS, Pielou's evenness, and ENS-based evenness — get_alpha_shannon
title: Calculate Shannon's entropy, ENS, Pielou's evenness, and ENS-based evenness — get_alpha_shannon
text: Shannon's H entropy (nats), diversity. SHANNON_ENS converts effective number species via exp(H) — q=1 Hill number, interpretable number equally abundant species produce entropy. Two evenness measures returned: Pielou's J (widely used, entropy-based) ENS-based evenness consistent Hill number framework.

title: Calculate Shannon's entropy, ENS, Pielou's evenness, and ENS-based evenness — get_alpha_shannon
Calculate Shannon's entropy, ENS, Pielou's evenness, and ENS-based evenness — get_alpha_shannon
text: df dataframe features rows samples columns feature_id name column feature IDs (default "feature_id") id_col name column sample IDs (default "SAMPLE")

title: Calculate Shannon's entropy, ENS, Pielou's evenness, and ENS-based evenness — get_alpha_shannon
text: tibble one row per sample columns: SHANNON_H Shannon entropy H (nats); index, diversity SHANNON_ENS Effective number species (exp(H)); q=1 Hill number SHANNON_PIELOU Pielou's J evenness (H / log(S.obs)); ratio observed maximum possible entropy SHANNON_EVENNESS ENS-based evenness (SHANNON_ENS / S.obs); ranges 1/S.obs (single dominant) 1 (species equal)

what: Calculate Simpson's diversity, Gini-Simpson index, ENS, and evenness — get_alpha_simpson
title: Calculate Simpson's diversity, Gini-Simpson index, ENS, and evenness — get_alpha_simpson
text: Computes four related measures Simpson's D (sum squared relative abundances). Note GINI_SIMPSON (1 - D) diversity index, evenness measure — probability two randomly drawn individuals belong different species. True evenness derived number species (SIMPSON_ENS).

title: Calculate Simpson's diversity, Gini-Simpson index, ENS, and evenness — get_alpha_simpson
Calculate Simpson's diversity, Gini-Simpson index, ENS, and evenness — get_alpha_simpson
text: df dataframe features rows samples columns feature_id name column feature IDs (default "feature_id") id_col name column sample IDs (default "SAMPLE")

title: Calculate Simpson's diversity, Gini-Simpson index, ENS, and evenness — get_alpha_simpson
text: tibble one row per sample columns: SIMPSON_D Simpson's dominance index (sum squared proportions); lower = diverse GINI_SIMPSON Gini-Simpson index (1 - D); diversity index, evenness SIMPSON_ENS Effective number species (1 / D); q=2 Hill number; interpretable number equally abundant dominant species yield D SIMPSON_ENS_EVENNESS ENS-based evenness (SIMPSON_ENS / S.obs); ranges 1/S.obs (single dominant) 1 (species equal)

title: Calculate Simpson's diversity, Gini-Simpson index, ENS, and evenness — get_alpha_simpson
text: SIMPSON_EVENNESS Previously 1 - SIMPSON_D (Gini-Simpson index). Renamed GINI_SIMPSON. name SIMPSON_EVENNESS longer returned — old workflows using error rather silently return wrong values. SIMPSON_INVERSE Renamed SIMPSON_ENS. Old workflows using SIMPSON_INVERSE error.

title: jakR2 0.4
text: SIMPSON_EVENNESS renamed GINI_SIMPSON (1 - D); SIMPSON_INVERSE renamed SIMPSON_ENS (1/D) New SIMPSON_ENS_EVENNESS column (ENS / S.obs) SHANNON_E renamed SHANNON_PIELOU; new SHANNON_EVENNESS column (ENS / S.obs) Added roxygen documentation package data objects Added @param entries clusters filter ft class Added testthat coverage alpha-diversity functions

title: jakR2 0.3
text: new S7 object called ft hold feature tables methods filtering based min count sample methods OTU clustering reusable regular expressions new functions work multiple seqtab objects (seqtab_stats_bind seqtab_stats_plot) better feature naming options ft objects new obs_callout() function split jak_theme() 6 smaller functions, jak_theme() now wrapper around

title: jakR2 0.2
text: new order parameter default, reverse random palette order blend_palette() use monochromeR::generate_palette() blending jak_theme() tweaks Alpha-diversity metrics Session package info utilities
